name: Analyze lab data quality issues and produce harmonized output with quality report
prompt: Read raw_labs.csv with clinical lab data containing various quality issues. Analyze and produce a data quality report. Steps: (1) identify rows with missing values (empty cells) and exclude them from analysis, (2) parse all numeric formats in the remaining rows: scientific notation (e.g. 1.5e0, 1.42e2), European decimals with comma (e.g. '88,4' means 88.4), and values with leading/trailing whitespace, (3) detect values needing unit conversion by checking against expected US conventional ranges — Serum_Creatinine 0.2-20.0 mg/dL (convert µmol/L by multiplying by 0.0113), Glucose 30-500 mg/dL (convert mmol/L by dividing by 0.0555), Hemoglobin 3-20 g/dL (convert g/L by multiplying by 0.1), Total_Cholesterol 50-400 mg/dL (convert mmol/L by dividing by 0.0259), Calcium 4-15 mg/dL (convert mmol/L by dividing by 0.2495), BUN 2-150 mg/dL (convert mmol/L by multiplying by 2.8), (4) convert all out-of-range values to US conventional units and round to 2 decimal places. Write quality_report.json with fields: total_rows (total rows in raw file), excluded_rows (count of rows with any missing value), issues_found (object with counts: mixed_units, scientific_notation, european_decimal, whitespace). Also write cleaned_labs.csv containing only the non-missing rows with all values harmonized.

criteria.description: Issue counts are non-zero and within expected ranges
Cleaned lab values are all within US conventional ranges
quality_report.json has required structure with total_rows=30
